emails.content:
THE MOTLEY FOOL | Tax Season Special

Your HSA Could Be Your Best Tax Tool

With the April 15 deadline approaching, here are 4 moves to make with your Health Savings Account:

1. TOP OFF YOUR 2025 CONTRIBUTIONS
You can still contribute to your 2025 HSA until April 15, 2026. The individual limit was $4,300. If you didn't max out through payroll, make up the difference now. Every dollar reduces your taxable income.

2. REVIEW YOUR INVESTMENT ALLOCATION
Money sitting in cash inside your HSA is losing value to inflation. Most HSA providers let you invest once you hit a minimum threshold. Target-date funds or total market index funds are popular choices.

3. DON'T FORGET THE SHOEBOX METHOD
Paid for medical expenses out of pocket? Save those receipts. You can reimburse yourself from your HSA at any time, even years later. There is no deadline.

4. CHECK YOUR 2026 CONTRIBUTION RATE
The 2026 limit increases to $4,400 for individual coverage. If your payroll deduction was set for the 2025 limit, update it now.

emails.subject: Fwd: Should You Max Out Your HSA? The Math Says Yes
emails.content:
ok so the answer is definitely yes. even $148 matters because of the triple tax advantage thing

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Investopedia <newsletter@investopedia.com>
Date: Mar 2, 2026
Subject: Should You Max Out Your HSA? The Math Says Yes

The Health Savings Account is the only account in the tax code with a triple tax advantage:

1. Contributions are tax-deductible (or pre-tax through payroll)
2. Growth is tax-free
3. Qualified withdrawals are tax-free

No 401(k), IRA, or Roth can match all three.

So should you max it out, even if it means stretching your budget? In almost every case, yes.

At a 22% marginal tax rate, a $4,300 contribution saves you $946 in federal taxes. If you're short by even $100, that's $22 you're leaving on the table.

The HSA also works as a stealth retirement account. After age 65, you can withdraw for any purpose without penalty (you just pay income tax, like a traditional IRA). Before 65, keep receipts for every medical expense and reimburse yourself decades later. The money grows tax-free in the meantime.

For 2025: Individual limit is $4,300. Family limit is $8,750. You have until April 15, 2026 to contribute.

Read more at investopedia.com/hsa-max-contribution-strategy

emails.content:
some good stuff in here. need to check the uniform deduction and CME thing

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Forbes <newsletters@forbes.com>
Date: Mar 4, 2026
Subject: 7 Tax Deductions Healthcare Workers Miss Every Year

If you work in healthcare, you might be leaving money on the table every April. Here are deductions many nurses, NPs, PAs, and techs overlook:

1. CONTINUING EDUCATION (CME)
Fees for required CE credits, conferences, and certifications are deductible if your employer doesn't reimburse them.

2. PROFESSIONAL MEMBERSHIPS
ANP, AANP, state nursing board dues, and specialty association fees.

3. UNIFORMS AND SCRUBS
If you buy your own scrubs and they're not suitable for everyday wear, the cost is deductible. This includes shoes designated for clinical use.

4. HSA CONTRIBUTIONS
Health Savings Account contributions reduce your taxable income dollar for dollar. Many healthcare workers undercontribute.

5. LICENSE RENEWAL FEES
State NP license, DEA registration, and national certification fees.

6. MALPRACTICE INSURANCE
If you carry your own professional liability policy (not covered by your employer), the premium is deductible.

7. HOME OFFICE (if applicable)
If you do administrative work, charting, or telehealth from a dedicated home space.

Note: Many of these deductions were suspended for W-2 employees under the 2017 tax reform. They may still apply if you have 1099 income or if your state allows them. Check with a tax professional.

Read more at forbes.com/advisor/taxes/healthcare-worker-deductions

---------- Forwarded message ----------
From: Texas Monthly <newsletters@texasmonthly.com>
Date: Mar 10, 2026
Subject: Container Gardening in Dallas: What to Plant Now

The best vegetables for Dallas balcony and patio containers, according to local master gardeners:

1. Cherry tomatoes (Sun Gold is king)
2. Jalapeños and serranos
3. Herbs: basil, cilantro, oregano, rosemary
4. Okra (yes, in containers, use 5-gallon pots minimum)
5. Bell and sweet peppers do great in 12" pots with full sun

Key dates for Zone 8a:
- Last average frost: March 17
- Start tomatoes indoors: 6-8 weeks before last frost
- Direct sow herbs after last frost

The #1 mistake container gardeners make in Dallas: underwatering in July and August. Self-watering pots or drip irrigation is basically required June through August.

Read more at texasmonthly.com/garden

529 Plan Tax Deductions by State: 2025 Guide

Over 30 states offer a state income tax deduction or credit for 529 plan contributions.

States with full deduction for any 529 plan: Arizona, Kansas, Maine, Missouri, Montana, Pennsylvania

States with deduction only for in-state plans: Most other states offering deductions

States with NO income tax (no deduction applicable): Alaska, Florida, Nevada, New Hampshire, South Dakota, Tennessee, Texas, Washington, Wyoming

If you live in Texas: No state tax deduction, but 529 plans still offer federal tax-free growth and tax-free withdrawals for qualified education expenses. The compounding benefit is significant over 10+ years.

2025 Gift Tax Exclusion: You can contribute up to $18,000 per beneficiary without triggering gift tax reporting.

emails.subject: North Texas Gardener: March Planting Guide + Zone 8a Tips
emails.content:
NORTH TEXAS GARDENER | March 14, 2026

MARCH PLANTING GUIDE: ZONE 8A

Spring is here, DFW! Here's what to get in the ground (or in pots) this week:

DIRECT SOW OUTDOORS:
Lettuce, spinach, radishes, peas, carrots

START INDOORS:
Tomatoes, peppers, eggplant (transplant outdoors after last frost, ~March 20)

CONTAINER TIP OF THE WEEK:
Self-watering planters are your best friend in Dallas summers. A 12" pot with drainage and a water reservoir will cut your watering time in half once July hits. Use a quality potting mix, not garden soil.

LOCAL SPOTLIGHT:
North Haven Gardens (7700 Northaven Rd) has their spring seed sale running through March 22. 20% off all seed packets.

PEST ALERT:
Aphids are already active on early-season greens. Neem oil spray early and often.

---
